title: Test public U-Boot SDMMC0 IRQ 40 against current diagnostic kernel
kernel_subject: arm64: dts: test A733 SDMMC0 IRQ 40
test_changes: Changed only SDMMC0 DTS interrupt from GIC_SPI 161 to GIC_SPI 40, matching the public U-Boot A733 DTS clue.
Kept the existing H027 diagnostic MMC driver, clocks, reset, descriptor geometry, and boot procedure.
key_evidence: Linux version 7.1.0-rc5-00283-g8d569cf9c19f booted on Machine model: Radxa Cubie A7S.
The DTB disassembled to interrupts = <0x00 0x28 0x04> for mmc@4020000.
sunxi-mmc 4020000.mmc initialized and reached the first CMD0 request.
CMD0 posted rint=0x00000004 and mista=0x00000004 with imask=0x0000bbc6, then did not progress to later card init, mmcblk, or the forced descriptor-fetch CMD18.
No DESC_READ, idma-post-cmdr, after-cpu-sync, CHDA/CBDA, or descriptor checksum evidence was produced in this run.
interpretation: IRQ 40 is not a usable fix for the current Linux diagnostic path. It regresses the boot before the known IDMAC descriptor-fetch stall, consistent with MMC completion interrupt delivery not matching the current kernel/DTB wiring.
current_blocker: A733 SDMMC0 IDMAC descriptor-fetch reachability remains the blocker. H035 closes the public U-Boot IRQ-40 clue as a runtime fix candidate for this exact v4/v5 diagnostic lineage.
next: Return to the prior stance: wait for maintainer/list feedback or a source-backed delta that names one concrete register, reset/clock ordering, firmware handoff, master ID, binding, or erratum before another behavior patch.
